SEARCH
Package edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue

Class HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue<K extends Comparable<K>,V>

java.lang.Object
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.AbstractPriorityQueue<K,V>
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.HeapPriorityQueue<K,V>
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K - the type of keys (priorities) stored in the adaptable priority queue
V - the type of values that are associated with keys in the adaptable priority queue
All Implemented Interfaces:
AdaptablePriorityQueue<K,V>, PriorityQueue<K,V>
public class HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue<K extends Comparable<K>,V> extends HeapPriorityQueue<K,V> implements AdaptablePriorityQueue<K,V>
A HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue is an array-based min-heap implementation of the AdaptablePriorityQueue abstract data type. HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue ensures a O(logn) worst-case runtime for PriorityQueue.insert(K, V), PriorityQueue.deleteMin(), AdaptablePriorityQueue.remove(edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.PriorityQueue.Entry<K, V>), and AdaptablePriorityQueue.replaceKey(edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.PriorityQueue.Entry<K, V>, K). HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue ensures a O(1) worst-case runtime for PriorityQueue.min(), PriorityQueue.size(), PriorityQueue.isEmpty(), and AdaptablePriorityQueue.replaceValue(edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.PriorityQueue.Entry<K, V>, V). The HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue class is based on an implementation developed for use with the textbook: Data Structures and Algorithms in Java, Sixth Edition Michael T. Goodrich, Roberto Tamassia, and Michael H. Goldwasser John Wiley and Sons, 2014
Author:
Dr. King, Diya Patel

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static class 
    HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue.AdaptablePQEntry<K,V>
    An AdaptablePQEntry extends AbstractPriorityQueue.PQEntry to maintain a reference of the entry's current index within the array-based heap data structure.

    AbstractPriorityQueue.NaturalOrder, AbstractPriorityQueue.PQEntry<K,V>

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.PriorityQueue

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue()
    Constructs a new HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue using the natural ordering of keys
    HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue(Comparator<K> comparator)
    Constructs a new HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue using a custom comparator

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    remove(PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> entry)
    The code for this method is based on the remove algorithm on page 391 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser.
    void
    replaceKey(PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> entry, K key)
    The code for this method is based on the replaceKey algorithm on page 391 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser.
    void
    replaceValue(PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> entry, V value)
    The code for this method is based on the replaceValue algorithm on page 391 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser.
    void
    swap(int index1, int index2)
    Swaps the entry at index1 with the entry at index2

    • HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue

      public HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue(Comparator<K> comparator)
      Constructs a new HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue using a custom comparator
      Parameters:
      comparator - the custom Comparator to use when comparing keys (priorities)

      public HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue()

  • Method Details

    • swap

      public void swap(int index1, int index2)
      Swaps the entry at index1 with the entry at index2
      Parameters:
      index1 - the index of the first entry involved in the swap
      index2 - the index of the second entry involved in the swap

    • remove

      public void remove(PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> entry)
      The code for this method is based on the remove algorithm on page 391 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. removes the entry given
